question: How did you actually get the plan coordinates out of Rowandoz? Did you go to a man named Hama Ali at a teahouse and recite them from memory, or did you carry a physical copy on leave to Sulaymaniyah and pass it through a messenger? Both? Something else?
answer:
Yes, I got the plan from Abdullah Shumkar. I took copies of the plans and went back to the city of Suleimaniya, where I sent them to our freedom fighters (the Peshmerga), so they have the plan already in their hands.At the time, I received the invitation because I thought they didn't have any connection with the Peshmerga yet, and I didn't want them to feel left behind. I told them: "This is an invitation for you to join the Peshmerga. If you don't come, you are still our friends and our brothers. If you do come, it is up to you."

Dr. Kamal Abdullah Naji, who was the dean, said, "I am more Kurd than you; I am coming." Dr. Abdul Qadir Shali, the head of the Suleimaniya Republic Hospital, also said, "I am coming; I am getting ready to join."

I arranged for both of them to leave. A group of Peshmerga came and took them to the city of Halabja two or three days later to join the forces.I delivered the letter to the doctors, especially Dr. Kamal Abdullah Naji (who was the Dean of Medical School) and Dr. Abdul Qadir Shali (who was the head of Sulaymaniyah Republic Hospital). 

I did that and I told them, "If you come, it's up to you. If you don't come, you are still our brother and it doesn't mean anything against you." But both of them said they were Kurds. Especially Dr. Kamal Abdullah Naji; he said at the time he was the Dean of Medical School, "I am more Kurdish than you, so I will come." 

I told him he was welcome, and I left them behind. Then I arranged for a messenger to take them by car to the city of Halabja.
